behaviors.description: Cell with =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to correct sum of range
behaviors.justification: The core failure—wrong summation—is a pure calculation bug detectable without I/O. Mock cells A1–A10 with known values, invoke SUM logic, verify result. No filesystem, subprocess, or LLM needed. MOCK-MISS? No—the arithmetic happens in-process regardless of how cells are stored.
behaviors.test_description: Set cells A1 through A10 to values [1, 2, 3, ..., 10], enter formula =SUM(A1:A10) in target cell, assert result equals 55. Include edge cases: negative values, zeros, single-cell range.

behaviors.description: Cascading formula references (=B1+1 where B1=A1*2) produce correct result
behaviors.justification: The failure is in formula dependency resolution and calculation order. This is deterministic, in-process logic. Mock cells A1, B1, C1; set A1=5, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+1; verify C1=11. No external I/O or coordination needed. MOCK-MISS? No—the dependency graph and evaluation are pure algorithms.
behaviors.test_description: Create three cells with dependency chain: A1=5, B1==A1*2 (should eval to 10), C1==B1+1 (should eval to 11). Verify C1 computes to 11 by resolving B1's value first. Test longer chains (4–5 levels) and verify order of evaluation.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1 refs B1, B1 refs A1) is detected and returns error
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection is a pure graph algorithm; failure is when the engine loops or misses the cycle. Unit test: create A1==B1, B1==A1; attempt evaluation; expect circular-ref error. This is algorithmic, no I/O or external coordination needed.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1==B1 and B1==A1, then attempt to evaluate either cell. Verify the engine returns a circular-reference error (not a timeout or stack overflow). Test longer cycles (A→B→C→A) and self-referential (A==A).

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; returns error on missing key
behaviors.justification: VLOOKUP is a built-in function; failure is wrong return value or missing-key error not raised. Unit test: mock a lookup table (as cells), call VLOOKUP with existing and missing keys, verify results. All in-process, no I/O. MOCK-MISS? No—the lookup logic is pure code.
behaviors.test_description: Create lookup table in cells (e.g., A1:B5 with names and IDs). Call VLOOKUP to find existing key, verify returns correct value. Call VLOOKUP with non-existent key, verify returns #N/A or error. Test approximate match=FALSE explicitly.

behaviors.description: IF function correctly evaluates nested conditions (e.g., =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, 'high', 'medium'), 'low'))
behaviors.justification: IF is a built-in conditional; failure is wrong branch taken or nesting not handled. Unit test: set cell values, evaluate nested IF, verify the correct branch result. All in-process logic, no external I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Test IF with A1=5, A1=15, A1=25. For each, verify correct branch is taken: 5→'low', 15→'medium', 25→'high'. Include edge cases (boundary values 10, 20) and deeply nested conditions (3+ levels).

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with separator, skips empty cells (no blank strings inserted)
behaviors.justification: CONCATENATE is a built-in string function; failure is incorrect separator handling or empty-cell insertion. Unit test: populate cells with mixed empty/non-empty values, call CONCATENATE, verify output. Pure string logic, no I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Set cells: A1='hello', A2=(empty), A3='world', A4='' (explicit empty). Call CONCATENATE with separator ','. Verify result is 'hello,world' (empty cells skipped, no extra commas). Test with various separators and all-empty ranges.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (e.g., =Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: The failure is in sheet lookup and reference parsing. Unit test: mock two sheets with proper names, place value in Sheet2.A1, reference it from Sheet1, verify formula evaluation. All in-process; sheet objects are mocked. MOCK-MISS? No—the name resolution and reference parsing are deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Create two sheets: Sheet1 and Sheet2. Put value 10 in Sheet2.A1. In Sheet1, enter formula =Sheet2!A1+1. Verify result is 11. Test missing sheet reference (should return error) and invalid syntax (should parse error).

behaviors.description: When referenced cell's value changes, dependent formulas recalculate in topological order
behaviors.justification: Real failure: cell changes but dependents don't update, or recalculation order is wrong (uses stale values). A unit test can mock cell change events and verify: (1) dependency graph is correct, (2) recalculation is triggered, (3) order respects dependencies. However, if change propagation involves an event system that could be broken, Integration becomes safer. MOCK-MISS? In-process mock of change + recalc detects the failure; event system bugs might slip past depending on test design. Borderline Unit/Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5. Create formulas: B1==A1+1 (expect 6), C1==B1+2 (expect 8), D1==A1+C1 (expect 13). Change A1 to 10. Verify: B1→11, C1→13, D1→23 (using new values, not stale). Log recalculation order; assert order respects dependencies (B1 and C1 before D1). Test diamond dependency (A1→B1, A1→C1, D1==B1+C1).

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores both formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: Failure: undo doesn't restore formula text, or dependent values are lost or recalculated incorrectly. Unit test can mock the undo stack and verify: (1) formula text is stored/restored, (2) dependent values before edit are preserved in undo record. However, if the system caches computed values and undo must restore them without recalculation, Integration testing ensures the cache is correctly managed. Borderline Unit/Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5, B1==A1+1 (B1=6). Edit B1 to ==A1+2. Verify B1=7. Call undo. Verify B1 formula restored to ==A1+1 AND B1 value restored to 6 (or recalculated to 6). Test undo with dependency chain: A1→B1→C1, edit A1, undo, verify all three restored to pre-edit state.

behaviors.description: Workbook saved to disk (binary format) and loaded back with no data loss or floating-point precision change
behaviors.justification: Real failures: serialization bug (wrong byte format), floating-point precision loss during round-trip, formula parsing error on load, or version incompatibility. Unit test with mocked I/O cannot catch these. MOCK-MISS? Absolutely—mocking the file system bypasses serialization bugs entirely. REAL-INFRA: The behavior requires actual binary serialization/deserialization. Integration level (local filesystem or in-memory file mock that captures actual bytes) is minimum to verify format integrity and floating-point round-trip.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook: A1=3.14159265359, B1==A1*2, C1='text'. Save to binary file. Load into fresh engine. Verify: A1 value matches (within floating-point tolerance), B1 formula and value correct, C1 text identical. Test with mixed types: dates, large numbers, special characters. Verify file size and binary format are consistent across save/load cycles.
